brief_document.title: Runtime discriminant and session lifecycle branching
brief_document.purpose: Introduce the RuntimeKind discriminant so that AssistantService can drive either Norn or Claude Runner behind a single session lifecycle contract. This is the foundation brief — every other brief in the cluster depends on the types and branching established here.
brief_document.task: Add RuntimeKind enum to types.rs, add runtime field to SessionConfig, store RuntimeKind on SessionHandle, add Norn error variants to AssistantError, and modify start_session to branch on RuntimeKind. The Claude Runner path remains unchanged. The Norn path is stubbed (spawn_norn_loop exists but delegates to NR-002 for implementation). Both paths converge on the same SessionRegistry and broadcast::Sender<AssistantEvent>.

requirements.title: RuntimeKind enum
requirements.spec: The system SHALL define a RuntimeKind enum with variants ClaudeRunner and Norn in types.rs. RuntimeKind SHALL derive Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize. RuntimeKind SHALL implement Default with ClaudeRunner as the default.
acceptance: RuntimeKind::ClaudeRunner and RuntimeKind::Norn are defined in types.rs
RuntimeKind derives Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize
RuntimeKind::default() returns ClaudeRunner

requirements.title: SessionConfig runtime field
requirements.spec: SessionConfig SHALL have a field `runtime: Option<RuntimeKind>` that defaults to None. WHEN runtime is None, the session SHALL use the server default runtime. WHEN runtime is Some(kind), the session SHALL use that runtime regardless of defaults.
acceptance: SessionConfig has runtime: Option<RuntimeKind> field
SessionConfig::default() has runtime: None
Existing SessionConfig construction sites compile without changes (field defaults to None)

requirements.title: SessionHandle stores RuntimeKind
requirements.spec: SessionHandle SHALL store the resolved RuntimeKind. SessionInfo (the public snapshot) SHALL expose the runtime_kind field so that WS handlers and API responses can report which runtime a session uses.
acceptance: SessionHandle has a runtime_kind: RuntimeKind field
SessionInfo has a runtime_kind: RuntimeKind field
SessionRegistry get() and list() return SessionInfo with populated runtime_kind

requirements.title: Norn error variants
requirements.spec: AssistantError SHALL have variants NornBuildFailed(String) and NornSessionFailed(String) for errors originating from the Norn runtime path. These SHALL NOT overlap with existing error variants.
acceptance: AssistantError::NornBuildFailed(String) is defined in error.rs
AssistantError::NornSessionFailed(String) is defined in error.rs
Both variants have Display implementations via thiserror

requirements.title: start_session branches on RuntimeKind
requirements.spec: WHEN start_session is called with a SessionConfig whose resolved runtime is ClaudeRunner, THE SYSTEM SHALL follow the existing Claude Runner path (build_command, spawn_session_loop). WHEN the resolved runtime is Norn, THE SYSTEM SHALL follow the Norn path (spawn_norn_loop). Both paths SHALL register a SessionHandle in the same SessionRegistry and produce a broadcast::Sender<AssistantEvent>.
acceptance: start_session resolves RuntimeKind from config.runtime (or server default if None)
ClaudeRunner path calls existing build_command and spawn_session_loop unchanged
Norn path calls spawn_norn_loop (stub in this brief, implemented in NR-002)
Both paths register a SessionHandle with the resolved RuntimeKind
Both paths create broadcast::channel<AssistantEvent>(256)

requirements.title: spawn_norn_loop stub
requirements.spec: A spawn_norn_loop method SHALL exist on AssistantService that accepts session_id, config, and RuntimeKind::Norn-specific artifacts. In this brief it SHALL return AssistantError::NornBuildFailed with a message indicating the Norn path is not yet implemented. NR-002 replaces the stub with the real implementation.
acceptance: spawn_norn_loop method exists on AssistantService
Calling spawn_norn_loop returns Err(AssistantError::NornBuildFailed(...))
The existing spawn_session_loop is renamed to spawn_claude_runner_loop for clarity

brief_document.dependency_setup: No new cargo dependencies. All required crates (serde, uuid, thiserror) are already in meridian-services Cargo.toml. The norn crate is also already a dependency (norn = { path = "../norn" }).
boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the Norn session loop (NR-002 scope)
SHALL NOT implement event translation (NR-003 scope)
SHALL NOT modify the Claude Runner session loop or translate layer
SHALL NOT add new crate dependencies to meridian-services Cargo.toml (norn is already present from builder work)
SHALL NOT modify ws/assistant.rs, persistence.rs, or streaming.rs
verification: cargo check --workspace passes with the new types
All existing tests pass unchanged (Claude Runner path unaffected)
SessionConfig with runtime: None resolves to the server default
SessionInfo for a Claude Runner session reports RuntimeKind::ClaudeRunner

checklist.text: RuntimeKind enum with ClaudeRunner and Norn variants defined in types.rs
SessionConfig has an optional runtime field of type Option<RuntimeKind>
SessionHandle stores RuntimeKind and is queryable via the registry
AssistantError has NornBuildFailed and NornSessionFailed variants
AssistantService::start_session branches on RuntimeKind after token acquisition
spawn_norn_loop method builds AgentBuilder, creates event channels, spawns async task
Existing spawn_session_loop (Claude Runner path) is unchanged and continues to work
Both runtime paths register a SessionHandle with the same SessionRegistry
Both runtime paths produce broadcast::Sender<AssistantEvent> for downstream consumers

stories.text: As a developer, I want to switch a member's runtime between Norn and Claude Runner via wake config so that I can migrate incrementally.
As a developer, I want both runtimes to produce the same event stream so that the frontend works identically regardless of runtime.
As a developer, I want to see which runtime a session is using so that I can diagnose runtime-specific issues.

constraints.text: No new external dependencies. Namespace tools call service-layer functions via ServiceContext, not HTTP.
AssistantEvent is the single event type for all downstream consumers. Both runtimes produce it.
JSONL session files are Norn's source of truth for resume. PG is the UI's source of truth. Neither replaces the other.
The model sees at most 25 tools (16 standard Norn + up to 7 namespace + 2 optional web). Profile filtering reduces this in practice.
Session IDs are UUIDs shared between the JSONL filename, PG assistant_session_events key, and SessionHandle registry key.
Wake prompt delivery mechanism changes, format does not.
mod.rs contains only pub mod declarations and re-exports.
No file over 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace).
All errors handled via thiserror in library code. No .unwrap() or .expect() in library code.
Namespace tools implement the existing Tool trait. No changes to the trait interface.

resolved_context.intention: Norn becomes the primary runtime for all member-facing work in Meridian. Every DM, wake prompt, inbox triage, and interactive assistant session runs through Norn in-process within the server. Claude Runner stays only for legacy background task dispatch until it can be retired entirely. Member-facing sessions run as typed, observable, in-process agent loops rather than opaque subprocess invocations. Session state persists across visits without relying on a CLI binary's internal persistence. The model sees a focused set of domain tools instead of a raw shell with 175 CLI commands. This is the June 15 deadline work.

provenance.quote: Norn becomes the primary runtime for all member-facing work in Meridian. Every DM, wake prompt, inbox triage, and interactive assistant session runs through Norn in-process within the server. Claude R
